title: aster alpinus subsp. glabratus
intro: Aster alpinus subsp. glabratus, also known as Alpine Aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Aster alpinus subsp. glabratus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
